tipRules.tip: Palaniuk: 'Fish the Neko rig in and around brushpiles and flip it to isolated trees where fish are suspended — use 360 imaging to see fish suspended around standing timber and specifically target trees with fish'
Palaniuk: 'Use a 3/16-oz VMC Half Moon weight with a 1/0 weedless Neko hook for fishing nasty cover — expect to lose some fish but you get far more bites than with football jigs or big cranks'
Palaniuk: 'Use 15-lb braid to 10-lb fluorocarbon leader on a 7-ft medium spinning rod for deep Neko rigging in cover'
Palaniuk: 'Throw a topwater walker on 40-lb braid with a medium rod that has a good tip — the braid requires a rod tip with enough give to walk the bait and create action'
Palaniuk: 'Start with a Texas-rigged 10-inch worm in brushpiles because the heavier line and bigger hook up the odds of landing fish in cover, then switch to Neko rig for more bites'
Palaniuk: 'Use 1/2-oz tungsten flipping weight with VMC Sinker Stop, 5/0 heavy duty wide gap hook, 15-17lb fluorocarbon for Texas-rigged 10-inch worm in deep cover'

knowledge.insight: Palaniuk: 'Big fish that haven't made the full transition to main summertime structure are singles or doubles on brushpiles or standing timber with a bottom composition change (mud to hard), on straight banks leading from spawning areas to points — a midway transition point'
Palaniuk: 'Standing-timber fish sit in 18-28ft suspended 5-6ft off the bottom, relating to bottom composition changes even though they aren't on the bottom'

Palaniuk: 'The same principles applied from 4ft to 30ft: it had to have a good straight steeper edge close to a main channel — main-lake fish near the main river channel, grass fish near a creek channel'
Palaniuk: 'Parallel the edges where grass falls off to a straight wall to keep the bait in the strike zone the longest — fish roam and sit on the edge for an ambush point'

Palaniuk: 'When marking fish on electronics that won't bite big cranks or football jigs, drop a Neko rig — the first fish was a 9+. When I kept it in my hand I could go to every single brushpile and catch one'
